qas.q:
(Spillage)
Which of the following is a good practice to prevent spillage?
qas.a: Always check to make sure you are using the correct network for the level of data

qas.q:
(Spillage)
What does "spillage" refer to?
qas.a: Information improperly moved from a higher protection level to a lower protection level

qas.q:
(Spillage)
Which of the following may help to prevent spillage?
qas.a: Follow procedures for transferring data to and from outside agency and non-Government networks

qas.q:
(Spillage)
A vendor conducting a pilot program with your organization contacts you for organizational data to use in a prototype. How should you respond?
qas.a: Refer the vendor to the appropriate personnel

qas.q:
(Spillage)
You receive an inquiry from a reporter about government information not cleared for public release. How should you respond?
qas.a: Refer to reporter to your organization's public affairs office

qas.q:
(Spillage)
You find information that you know to be classified on the Internet. What should you do?
qas.a: Note the website's URL and report the situation to your security point of contact

qas.q:
(Classified Data)
Who designates whether information is classification level?
qas.a: Original classification authority

qas.q:
(Classified Data)
Which of the following must you do before using an unclassified laptop and peripherals in a collateral classified environment?
qas.a: Ensure that any cameras, microphones, and Wi-Fi embedded in the laptop are physically disabled

qas.q:
(Classified Data)
Which of the following is a good practice to protect classified information?
qas.a: Don't assume open storage in a secure facility is authorized

qas.q:
(Classified Data)
What is the basis for the handling and storage of classified data?
qas.a: Classification markings and handling caveats

qas.q:
(Insider Threat)
Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? A colleague abruptly becomes hostile and unpleasant after previously enjoying positive working relationship with peers, purchases an unusually expensive new car, and has unexplained absences from work.
qas.a: 3 or more indicators

qas.q:
(Insider Threat)
Which scenario might indicate a reportable insider threat?
qas.a: A colleague removes sensitive information without seeking authorization in order to perform authorized telework.

qas.q:
(Insider Threat)
Which of the following is a reportable insider threat activity?
qas.a: Attempting to access sensitive information without need-to-know

qas.q:
(Insider Threat)
Which of the following is a potential insider threat indicator?
qas.a: Unusual interest in classified information

qas.q:
(Insider Threat)
What is an insider threat?
qas.a: Someone who uses authorized access, wittingly or unwittingly, to harm national security through unauthorized disclosure or other actions that may cause the loss or degradation of resources or capabilities.

qas.q:
(Social Networking)
How can you protect your organization on social networking sites?
qas.a: Ensure there are no identifiable landmarks visible in any photos taken in a work setting that you post

qas.q:
(Social Networking)
Which of the following statements is true?
qas.a: Adversaries exploit social networking sites to disseminate fake news.

qas.q:
(Social Networking)
When may you be subject to criminal, disciplinary, and/or administrative action due to online harassment, bullying, stalking, hazing, discrimination, or retaliation?
qas.a: If you participate in or condone it at any time

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which of the following is true of Protected Health Information (PHI)?
qas.a: It is created or received by a healthcare provider, health plan, or employer.

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which is a best practice for protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
qas.a: Store it in a locked desk drawer after working hours

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which designation marks information that does not have potential to damage national security?
qas.a: Unclassified

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which of the following is NOT an example of Personally Identifiable Information (PII)?
qas.a: High school attended

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which of the following is true of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
qas.a: CUI must be handled using safeguarding or dissemination controls.

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which designation includes Personally Identifiable Information (PlI) and Protected
Health Information (PHI)?
qas.a: Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)

qas.q:
(Controlled Unclassified Information)
Which of the following is a security best practice for protecting Personally Identifiable
Information (PII)?
qas.a:
Only use Government-furnished or Government-approved equipment to process
PIl.

qas.q:
(Physical Security)
Which of the following is a best practice for physical security?
qas.a: Report suspicious activity

qas.q:
(Physical Security)
Which of the following best describes good physical security?
qas.a: Lionel stops an individual in his secure area who is not wearing a badge.

qas.q:
(Identity Management)
Which of the following is true of using a DoD Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) token?
qas.a: It should only be in a system while actively using it for a PKI-required task.

qas.q:
(Identity Management)
Which of the following is true of the Common Access Card (CAC)?
qas.a: It contains certificates for identification, encryption, and digital signature.

qas.q:
(Identity Management)
Which of the following is an example of two-factor authentication?
qas.a: A Common Access Card and Personal Identification Number

qas.q:
(Identity Management)
What is the best way to protect your Common Access Card (CAC) or Personal Identity
Verification (PIV) card?
qas.a: Store it in a shielded sleeve

qas.q:
(Physical Security)
Which Cyber Protection Condition (CPCON) establishes a protection priority focus on critical functions only?
qas.a: CPCON 1

qas.q:
(Sensitive Compartmented Information)
What must authorized personnel do before permitting another individual to enter a
Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Confirm the individual's need-to-know and access

qas.q:
(Sensitive Compartmented Information)
Which of the following is true of sharing information in a Sensitive Compartmented
Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Individuals must avoid referencing derivatively classified reports classified higher than the recipient.

qas.q:
(Sensitive Compartmented Information)
Which of the following is true of Security Classification Guides?
qas.a: The provide guidance on reasons for and duration of classification of information.

qas.q:
(Removable Media in a SCIF)
Which of the following is true of portable electronic devices (PEDs) in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Only connect government-owned PEDs to the same level classification information system when authorized

qas.q:
(Removable Media in SCIF)
Which of the following is NOT a potential consequence of using removable media unsafely in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Damage to the removable media

qas.q:
(Malicious Code)
Which of the following is a way to prevent the spread of malicious code?
qas.a: Scan all external files before uploading to your computer

qas.q:
(Malicious Code)
Which of the following is NOT a type of malicious code?
qas.a: Executables

qas.q:
(Website Use)
Which of the following actions ean help to protect your identity?
qas.a: Shred personal documents

qas.q:
(Social Engineering)
What type of social engineering targets senior officials?
qas.a: Whaling

qas.q:
(Social Engineering)
Which of the following is true?
qas.a: Digitally signed e-mails are more secure.

qas.q:
(Social Engineering)
Which is an appropriate use of govemment e-mail?
qas.a: Use a digital signature when sending attachments or hyperlinks

qas.q:
(Travel)
What security risk does a public Wi-Fi connection pose?
qas.a: It may expose the information sent to theft.

qas.q:
(Use of GFE)
Which of the following represents an ethical use of your Government-furnished equipment (GFE)?
qas.a: Checking personal e-mail when allowed by your organization

qas.q:
(Mobile Devices)
How can you protect data on your mobile computing and portable electronic devices (PEDs)?
qas.a: Enable automatic screen locking after a pekod of inactivity

qas.q:
(Mobile Devices)
Which of the following is an example of removable media?
qas.a: Flash Drive / External hard drive

qas.q:
(Home Computer Security)
How should you secure your home wireless network for teleworking?
qas.a: Implement Wi-Fi Protected Access 2 (WPA2) Personal encryption at a minimum
